claim: The blocking logic for missing evidence, clarify recommendations, block recommendations, and failed evidence agent status is ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED in the YOLO demand router. Both explorer and cross-checker independently verified the implementation at src/demand/router.ts:481-511 with test coverage at __tests__/demand-router.test.ts:246-286. The demand describes existing functionality, not a gap requiring implementation.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() implements blocking for missing (496-501), clarify/block/failed (503-509)
evidence.why: Core implementation verified by both agents; all demand-specified blocking conditions are present

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues() for each agent result
evidence.why: Integration point verified; blocking issues are collected into blockerList

evidence.summary: prd_ready: blockers.length === 0 ensures any blocker prevents readiness
evidence.why: Final gatekeeper verified; evidence agent blockers correctly prevent prd_ready=true

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence agent missing data and clarify recommendations block PRD readiness' with assertions for prd_ready=false and blocker presence
evidence.why: Test coverage independently verified by both agents; proves blocking logic works

evidence.summary: Test 'cross-check PRD readiness requires every evidence agent result' verifies all three agents must complete
evidence.why: Cross-checker verified complete evidence agent workflow and blocker integration

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() sets status='failed', recommendation='block', verdict='blocked' on failure
evidence.why: Cross-checker verified failed agent normalization triggers blocking logic

risks: Demand may be misinterpreted as requesting new implementation when functionality already exists
If demand is reporting a specific bug, no evidence suggests current implementation is broken
Proceeding with implementation would create duplicate/unnecessary code

result.notes: Verifier confirms all evidence from explorer and cross-checker is well-sourced, specific, and factual. No assumptions promoted to facts. All paths are valid and line ranges are precise. Missing arrays are empty because all evidence was found. Both agents independently reached the same conclusion: the blocking logic requested in the demand is already implemented and tested. Demand intent requires clarification: is this (1) an audit/verification task (VERIFIED PASS), (2) request for additional edge-case test coverage, or (3) report of a specific bug scenario not covered by current implementation? Recommendation=clarify because proceeding with implementation would be redundant.
